recent_label_changes: Warnings: Acute Kidney Injury Due to Volume Depletion (5.5) — 10/2025

approved_indications.indication_text: Adjunct to diet and exercise to improve glycemic control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus.

To reduce the risk of major adverse cardiovascular events (CV death, non-fatal myocardial infarction, or non-fatal stroke) in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus who are at high risk for these events.
approved_indications.population: Adults with T2DM at high cardiovascular risk

not_approved_for: Pediatric patients (safety and effectiveness not established)
Weight management as primary indication (see Wegovy for that indication)

mechanism_of_action.moa_summary: Semaglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist with 94% sequence homology to human GLP-1. It selectively binds and activates the GLP-1 receptor.
key_mechanisms: Minor delay in early postprandial gastric emptying
Long half-life (~1 week) via albumin binding — reduces renal clearance and protects against DPP-4 degradation

approved_claims.claim_text: OZEMPIC (semaglutide) 14 mg reduced HbA1c by 1.4% from baseline vs. -0.3% for placebo in a 26-week monotherapy trial.
approved_claims.supporting_data: Trial 1 (NCT02906930), N=175 (semaglutide 14mg) vs N=178 (placebo), 26-week double-blind trial

approved_claims.claim_text: 77% of patients treated with OZEMPIC 14 mg achieved HbA1c < 7% in a 26-week monotherapy trial vs. 31% on placebo.
OZEMPIC 14 mg demonstrated superior HbA1c reduction (-1.3%) vs. empagliflozin 25 mg (-0.9%) in patients on background metformin.
OZEMPIC reduced the risk of major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE) in adults with type 2 diabetes at high CV risk.
approved_claims.supporting_data: 4-year CV outcomes trial (Trial 7), N=4,825 semaglutide vs N=4,825 placebo

approved_claims.claim_text: OZEMPIC 14 mg demonstrated non-inferior HbA1c reduction vs. liraglutide 1.8 mg subcutaneous injection.
OZEMPIC 14 mg produced mean body weight reduction of 3.7 kg from baseline at 26 weeks in monotherapy use.
approved_claims.supporting_data: Trial 1, mean baseline weight 88.1 kg, difference from placebo -2.3 kg [-3.1, -1.5]

contraindications.text: Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) or Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia syndrome type 2 (MEN 2).
Prior serious hypersensitivity reaction to semaglutide or any excipients in OZEMPIC/RYBELSUS tablets.

boxed_warning.warning_summary: In rodents, semaglutide causes dose-dependent thyroid C-cell tumors at clinically relevant exposures. Human relevance is unknown. Contraindicated in patients with personal/family history of MTC or MEN 2.

key_warnings: Acute Pancreatitis — observe for signs/symptoms; discontinue if suspected
Diabetic Retinopathy Complications — monitor patients with history of diabetic retinopathy
Hypoglycemia with insulin secretagogues or insulin — dose reduction may be required
Acute Kidney Injury Due to Volume Depletion — monitor renal function
Severe Gastrointestinal Adverse Reactions — NOT recommended in patients with severe gastroparesis
Hypersensitivity Reactions — anaphylaxis and angioedema reported
Acute Gallbladder Disease — gallbladder studies indicated if cholelithiasis or cholecystitis suspected
Pulmonary Aspiration During General Anesthesia — delayed gastric emptying; instruct patients to inform HCPs before surgeries

ozempic_tablets.starting: 1.5 mg orally once daily for 30 days (not effective for glycemic control at this dose)
ozempic_tablets.escalation: Days 31-60: increase to 4 mg once daily
ozempic_tablets.administration: Once daily on empty stomach, morning, with up to 4 oz water only. Wait 30 min before eating/drinking. Swallow whole.

rybelsus_tablets.starting: 3 mg orally once daily for 30 days
rybelsus_tablets.escalation: Days 31-60: increase to 7 mg once daily

dosing.note: RYBELSUS and OZEMPIC tablets are NOT substitutable on a mg-to-mg basis.

key_interactions: Insulin secretagogues (sulfonylureas) or insulin — increased hypoglycemia risk; dose reduction may be needed
Levothyroxine — total thyroxine AUC increased 33% with concomitant use; monitor
Narrow therapeutic index drugs — gastric emptying delay; consider increased monitoring

mandatory_references: All efficacy claims must reference specific clinical trial data (trial number, population, duration)
CV outcomes claim requires citing 4-year cardiovascular outcomes trial context
Body weight change must note it is not an approved weight management indication
prohibited_claims: Do NOT claim efficacy in pediatric patients — not established
Do NOT compare head-to-head to WEGOVY (different product/indication) without clear differentiation
Do NOT minimize or omit the boxed warning for thyroid C-cell tumor risk in any promotional materials
required_fair_balance: GI adverse reactions profile (nausea up to 20%)
